I need to produce a report of the latest AI news headlines.
- We're probably going to want to search the internet for up to date ai news headlines and articles.
- We'll need to read each of the articles into the scratchpad, creating a markdown block for each article (or discarding it if it doesn't have enough content of match the \"ai headlines\" criteria).

Note:
- Kiwix and wikipedia are encyclopedia reference sources, they don't contain any \"headlines\" or \"news\". We cannot use kiwix or wikipedia as part of this task. They are prohibited, even when a web search fails.
- DuckDuckGo can't handle an ISO date like 2026-04-04, and we might not find a given result on a given day, we just want things that are up to date. So don't use an explicit date in the web search.
- Try loading the file ai-sites.json for a number of starting points in finding news articles.
- You'll often load a page that is a landing page, linking to articles rather than containing the content itself. In these instance you need to drill down through the layers of the website extracting the links and downloading each further page. Only go 3 layers deep.

Output:
- The output needs to be in a markdown file located at ./data/{todays date}/ai-headlines.md
- The report needs to contain up to 10 de-duplicated sections.
- Each section should be around 500 words.

Housekeeping:
- After outputting the report, save an updated version of ai-sites.json with successful sites at the top, and unsuccessful ones at the bottom. If a web search higlighted a new source, add that to the list.
